name: Ice Cream Cake Roll
recipeIngredient: 2 squares (1 oz each) unsweetened baking chocolate
2/3 cup evaporated milk, heated to 160 to 170°F
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg yolks on high for 3 minutes or until light and fluffy.
2. Gradually add sugar and vanilla, beating until thick and lemon-colored.
3. Combine flour, cocoa, and baking powder; gradually add to egg yolk mixture. Beat on low until well mixed (mixture will be thick).
4. Beat egg whites and salt until soft peaks form. Fold a small amount into batter until no streaks of white remain; add the remaining egg whites.
5. Grease a 15-in x 10-in x 1-in pan. Line with waxed paper and grease and flour paper.
6. Spread batter evenly in pan.
7. Bake at 350°F for 15 minutes or until cake springs back when lightly touched.
8. Turn out onto a linen towel dusted with confectioners' sugar. Peel off paper and roll up with the towel.
9. Cool for 30 minutes.
10. Unroll cake; spread with ice cream to within 1 in of edges. Roll up again (this time without the towel inside).
11. Cover with plastic wrap and freeze until serving.
FOR THE CHOCOLATE SAUCE:
1. In the top of a double boiler over hot water, melt chocolate and butter.
2. Gradually add warm milk and sugar; stir constantly for 5 minutes or until completely dissolved.
3. Spoon over slices of cake.
